message:
fcp-e2e: wire e2e-full into concrete connector feature aggregation

## Why

Until now, every per-connector feature flag in fcp-e2e/Cargo.toml
was an empty list (`openai = []`, `slack = []`, `qdrant = []`, etc.)
and `e2e-full` was itself `[]`. That meant:

  - `cargo test -p fcp-e2e --features e2e-full` actually enabled
    nothing; any harness gated on `#[cfg(feature = "openai")]` was
    invisible.
  - There was no default at all (`default = []`), so an unqualified
    `cargo test -p fcp-e2e` compiled the shell of the crate and
    silently excluded every connector-specific compliance suite —
    including the google-docs and google-sheets harnesses that
    just landed in 575952b8.

The feature table was effectively decorative. This commit makes it
real.

## Change

  - Makes `default = ["e2e-full"]` so the plain `cargo test` /
    `cargo build -p fcp-e2e` now exercises the full connector
    matrix, matching developer expectation.
  - Expands `e2e-full` into an explicit list of every individual
    connector feature: the existing ~40 plus the two new ones
    (`google_docs`, `google_sheets`) so they participate in
    e2e-full alongside `google_drive` / `google_calendar`.
  - Leaves each per-connector feature as `= []` at the bottom so
    a developer can still opt in to a narrow subset (e.g.
    `--no-default-features --features "qdrant,stripe"`).

## Cargo.lock

Pairs with the Cargo.lock delta in this commit: `fcp-google-docs`
and `fcp-google-sheets` are now declared dependencies of the
fcp-e2e dev-dep graph, so Cargo's resolver records them in the
aggregator crate's dep list (lines 3109+). No version bumps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
